features.reason: Rendered Helm values and install scripts install or update the Cilium chart.
Controlled through Cilium Helm values; changing this can replace dataplane service handling.
Default rendered values use Kubernetes IPAM and allow explicit overrides.
Supported as a Helm values override; changing pod CIDR allocation is disruptive.
Supported as a Helm values override with cloud-profile preflight warnings.
Rendered Cilium values can enable native routing; changing routing mode is disruptive.
Rendered Cilium values can select tunnel mode; changing routing mode is disruptive.
Hubble is enabled in the default Cilium values.
Hubble Relay is enabled in the default Cilium values.
Rendered values accept Hubble UI overrides; the feature is applied through the Cilium chart.
Metrics are enabled in Helm values and validated by live service-proxy probes.
Rendered sample policy assets and scoped kubectl apply support this surface.
Rendered policy lifecycle assets include Cilium CRD policy examples.
Supported through Cilium policy values and optional Enterprise DNSProxy HA.
Cilium Envoy and Hubble metric values are rendered and validated.
Scoped apply validates Cilium Helm values for Gateway API enablement.
Scoped apply validates Cilium Helm values for ingress controller enablement.
Service mesh options are Cilium Helm-value driven and represented in the feature matrix.
Cluster Mesh rewiring is planned through CLI commands and requires the disruptive-change gate.
Scoped Cilium Helm values enablement and policy apply are covered.
Scoped apply enables the BGP control plane. Customer-specific BGP peer CRs must still be reviewed and supplied separately.
Rendered CRD examples and scoped kubectl apply cover LB IPAM pools.
L2 advertisement changes can affect service reachability and require the disruptive-change gate.
Encryption is Helm-value driven and disruptive when enabled or changed.
IPsec enablement is supported through Helm values and secret-file handoff.
Host firewall enablement is powerful enough to require the disruptive-change gate.
Bandwidth manager options are handled through Cilium Helm values.
Enterprise DNSProxy has a rendered private-repo chart path and values file.
Private chart access must be verified through helm repo/chart access before rendering an apply command.
Timescape is rendered only when Enterprise chart access is available or explicitly requested.
Rendered Helm values and install scripts install the public Tetragon chart.
Enterprise edition uses the Isovalent chart and requires license-file gating at apply time.
Rendered starter TracingPolicy and runtime policy lifecycle support kubectl apply/remove.
TracingPolicyNamespaced examples are included in the runtime policy bundle.
Tetragon event settings are rendered through Helm values.
Network events are enabled by default in the rendered values.
Runtime policy bundles include file-event examples.
Runtime policy bundles include syscall/kprobe examples.
The default runtime policy bundle is observe-only. Enforcement actions can block workloads and require a customer-approved policy spec before apply.
Default render uses file export for Splunk OTel filelog pickup.
Stdout export is available as a Helm values override for restricted clusters.
Rendered with an explicit deprecation warning because fluent-plugin-splunk-hec was archived on 2025-06-24.
Live validation probes Tetragon service metrics through the Kubernetes API server.
Live modes discover tetra availability and record the operator CLI path.
Runtime policy apply and removal runbooks are rendered as Kubernetes manifests and commands.
Curated bundles are tracked as a product surface, but this skill only renders observe-only starter policies unless the operator supplies an approved bundle.
Public chart values are not sufficient for automated install; the skill renders a deterministic customer-doc/chart-access workflow instead of omitting the product.
Cilium LB IPAM resources are rendered and can back the load-balancer workflow where supported.
BGP and L2 advertisement support exists through Cilium; private Isovalent LB packaging remains gated.

features.feature: Splunk Observability and Splunk Platform telemetry wiring
features.reason: Telemetry is delegated to splunk-observability-isovalent-integration and Cisco Security Cloud setup skills.

generic.supported_install_path: Helm install into an existing Kubernetes cluster with Cilium privileges.
cloud_cni_conflicts: unknown existing CNI must be inventoried before replacing
generic.kube_proxy_handling: Default values use strict kube-proxy replacement; set explicit values for partial replacement.
generic.ipam_constraints: Kubernetes IPAM by default; validate pod CIDRs before native routing or cluster-scope IPAM.
generic.kernel_ebpf_requirements: Linux kernel 5.10 or newer recommended for the rendered Cilium and Tetragon paths.
generic.lb_ipam_limitations: LB IPAM requires non-overlapping pools and an advertisement mechanism.

kubeadm.supported_install_path: Helm install after kubeadm networking bootstrap is ready.
kubeadm.kube_proxy_handling: Can replace kube-proxy when API server and node routing prerequisites are met.
kubeadm.ipam_constraints: Kubernetes or cluster-scope IPAM; confirm kubeadm podSubnet.
kubeadm.kernel_ebpf_requirements: Kernel 5.10+ with cgroup v2/eBPF support where possible.
kubeadm.lb_ipam_limitations: Use BGP or L2 announcements for bare-metal services.

kind.supported_install_path: Lab-only Helm install; use reduced resource settings.
preflight_checks: kind config disables default CNI when replacing
kind.kube_proxy_handling: Kube-proxy replacement is possible only with matching kind network settings.
kind.ipam_constraints: Kubernetes IPAM; native routing is usually lab-only.
kind.lb_ipam_limitations: LoadBalancer tests need cloud-provider-kind, MetalLB, or equivalent.

minikube.supported_install_path: Lab-only Helm install with CNI replacement selected at cluster creation.
minikube.kube_proxy_handling: Use explicit minikube CNI/kube-proxy settings before install.
minikube.lb_ipam_limitations: Service exposure is lab-specific; validate tunnel or MetalLB behavior.

kops.supported_install_path: Helm install into a kOps cluster configured for Cilium.
kops.kube_proxy_handling: Plan kube-proxy replacement with kOps networking settings.
kops.ipam_constraints: Cloud ENI or Kubernetes IPAM depending on kOps cluster design.
kops.kernel_ebpf_requirements: Use recent distro kernels on node images.
kops.lb_ipam_limitations: Cloud load balancer and Cilium LB IPAM ownership must be explicit.

eks.supported_install_path: Helm install for supported EKS Cilium paths; prefer BYOCNI for CNI replacement.
eks.kube_proxy_handling: Kube-proxy replacement requires explicit planning with EKS add-ons.
eks.ipam_constraints: AWS ENI, Kubernetes, or BYOCNI-specific IPAM depending on cluster mode.
eks.kernel_ebpf_requirements: Amazon Linux or Bottlerocket kernels with eBPF support.
eks.lb_ipam_limitations: AWS Load Balancer Controller ownership must not conflict with Cilium LB IPAM.

eks-byocni.supported_install_path: Create EKS with --network-plugin none, then install Cilium before workloads.
cloud_cni_conflicts: AWS VPC CNI must not be active
eks-byocni.kube_proxy_handling: Kube-proxy add-on can be retained or replaced according to Cilium values.
eks-byocni.ipam_constraints: Use Cilium-supported IPAM; validate pod CIDRs and ENI expectations.
eks-byocni.kernel_ebpf_requirements: Kernel 5.10+; EKS Hybrid Nodes have distro exclusions called out in preflight.
eks-byocni.lb_ipam_limitations: Coordinate with AWS LBC and VPC routing before enabling service advertisements.

eks-hybrid.supported_install_path: Use the AWS EKS mirror OCI chart when required by the hybrid node support path.
eks-hybrid.kube_proxy_handling: Follow EKS hybrid guidance before strict replacement.
eks-hybrid.ipam_constraints: Validate hybrid node CIDR and routing ownership.
eks-hybrid.kernel_ebpf_requirements: Kernel 5.10+; avoid distro/kernel combinations excluded by EKS hybrid guidance.
eks-hybrid.lb_ipam_limitations: Hybrid routing needs site-specific BGP/L2 planning.

aks-byocni.supported_install_path: AKS cluster created for BYOCNI, then Cilium Helm install.
aks-byocni.kube_proxy_handling: Plan kube-proxy replacement with AKS networking mode.
aks-byocni.ipam_constraints: Validate Azure subnet and Cilium IPAM compatibility.
aks-byocni.kernel_ebpf_requirements: AKS node image kernel must support eBPF features used by Cilium/Tetragon.
aks-byocni.lb_ipam_limitations: Azure Load Balancer ownership may make LB IPAM not applicable.

aks-managed-cilium.supported_install_path: Discover and validate managed Cilium; do not replace the managed dataplane through this skill.
cloud_cni_conflicts: managed AKS networking owns the CNI
aks-managed-cilium.kube_proxy_handling: Managed by AKS; discover-only unless Microsoft-supported toggles are used.
required_privileges: cluster-admin only for Tetragon add-on install
scc_psa_rbac_requirements: PSA exemptions for Tetragon if installed separately
aks-managed-cilium.kernel_ebpf_requirements: Managed node images must support required eBPF features.
aks-managed-cilium.lb_ipam_limitations: Azure-managed load balancer path usually owns service allocation.

gke.supported_install_path: Use Cilium Helm only on clusters where GKE networking ownership permits replacement.
gke.kube_proxy_handling: GKE-managed modes may not allow replacement.
gke.ipam_constraints: Alias IP/native VPC constraints must be reviewed.
gke.kernel_ebpf_requirements: COS/Ubuntu node image eBPF support required.
gke.lb_ipam_limitations: GCP load balancer controller ownership can supersede LB IPAM.

gke-dataplane-v2.supported_install_path: Discover and validate managed Cilium dataplane; do not Helm-replace it.
required_privileges: cluster-admin only for separate Tetragon install where allowed
scc_psa_rbac_requirements: PSA exemptions for Tetragon if installed
gke-dataplane-v2.lb_ipam_limitations: GKE service load-balancer ownership usually makes Cilium LB IPAM not applicable.

openshift.supported_install_path: Render SCC/RBAC assets and use OpenShift-aware Helm values; CNI replacement requires platform review.
openshift.kube_proxy_handling: Do not replace without OpenShift networking review.
openshift.ipam_constraints: OpenShift network operator owns cluster CIDRs unless BYOCNI/custom networking is approved.
openshift.kernel_ebpf_requirements: RHCOS kernel must support required eBPF helpers.
openshift.lb_ipam_limitations: OpenShift Routes/Ingress and cloud load balancer ownership must be reviewed.

rke2.supported_install_path: RKE2 Cilium CNI or Helm install with bundled CNI disabled.
rke2.kube_proxy_handling: Configure RKE2 kube-proxy/CNI flags before strict replacement.
rke2.ipam_constraints: Kubernetes or cluster-scope IPAM; validate RKE2 cluster CIDR.
rke2.lb_ipam_limitations: MetalLB or Cilium LB IPAM ownership must be explicit.

rancher.supported_install_path: Rancher-managed Kubernetes with CNI ownership reviewed before Helm install.
rancher.kube_proxy_handling: Plan replacement through cluster provisioning settings.
scc_psa_rbac_requirements: Rancher PSA/project exemptions for privileged workloads
rancher.lb_ipam_limitations: Rancher service LB/MetalLB ownership must not conflict.

k3s.supported_install_path: Install with flannel disabled or use supported Cilium replacement flow.
k3s.kube_proxy_handling: Configure k3s flags for kube-proxy replacement.
k3s.ipam_constraints: Kubernetes IPAM; validate k3s cluster CIDR.
k3s.kernel_ebpf_requirements: Lightweight node OS kernel must support eBPF.
k3s.lb_ipam_limitations: Disable or coordinate klipper-lb before Cilium LB ownership.

k0s.supported_install_path: Install Cilium as the selected k0s network provider or Helm install after disabling conflicting CNI.
k0s.kube_proxy_handling: Configure k0s networking before strict replacement.
k0s.ipam_constraints: Kubernetes or cluster-scope IPAM; validate cluster CIDR.

talos.supported_install_path: Talos cluster with CNI disabled or Cilium selected, then Helm install through Kubernetes API.
cloud_cni_conflicts: Talos configured CNI if not Cilium
talos.kube_proxy_handling: Use Talos machine config to align kube-proxy replacement.
talos.ipam_constraints: Validate cluster/network CIDRs in Talos config.
required_privileges: Talos config access for node-level changes
talos.kernel_ebpf_requirements: Talos kernel must expose required eBPF capabilities.
talos.lb_ipam_limitations: Use BGP/L2 with explicit node network planning.

vmware-vsphere.supported_install_path: Helm install on vSphere Kubernetes where privileged CNI replacement is allowed.
vmware-vsphere.ipam_constraints: Validate pod/service CIDRs and NSX ownership.
vmware-vsphere.lb_ipam_limitations: NSX ALB/AVI ownership may conflict with Cilium LB IPAM.

alibaba-ack.supported_install_path: ACK cluster with cloud CNI ownership reviewed before Cilium Helm install.
cloud_cni_conflicts: Terway or other ACK managed CNI
alibaba-ack.kube_proxy_handling: Plan replacement with ACK network mode support.
alibaba-ack.ipam_constraints: Cloud ENI/VPC constraints must be reviewed.
alibaba-ack.kernel_ebpf_requirements: ACK node image kernel must support eBPF.
alibaba-ack.lb_ipam_limitations: Alibaba Cloud LB ownership may make Cilium LB IPAM not applicable.
